summary: "Configuration should be a dependency from AST"
description: """This issue was created as derived from MULE-18312.      From MULE-18312 we found that in some scenarios _*Configuration*_ component definition is not properly loaded causing unexpected behaviour e.g _LazyMuleArtifactContext ignores custom Global Error Handler definition when this configuration is located after a failing flow_.    *Configuration* component definition is one of core's component and should be always enabled to ensure it will be loaded at first independently wherever it's located in mule's application definition.    To ensure this behavior we set _alwaysEnabled = true_ at parsers definitions _(See: CoreComponentBuildingDefinitionProvider.getComponentBuildingDefinitions)_    This property has impact on how application components are initialized especially in which order. (See: MuleArtifactContext.createApplicationComponents)    Even though this fix the issue there are some aspects to be re-evaluated:      [~elrodro83] suggested:   _Maybe a better approach is to make explicit the dependency from the flows to the global error handler on the AST?_         [~gsfernandes] said:  _Would be better to move out from the parsers definitions if we do this in AST. Which is the source of true for the dependencies on components. This will also mean that mule-configuration will comes first before any flow, right? As alwaysEnabled has also impact in the order in which components are initialized. I think that spring-config also makes use of this._    _How that would work? Would be need add a custom logic to support these types? We have to review as there were a few usages of """"alwaysEnabled"""" at parsers definition. We need to also check if other plugins that define their parsers if they are using this or not..._    _I would like to think this perhaps in the extension model if not how we can handle custom parsers?_  _If this is the way to go we would need to remove the explicit sorting that it is being done in MuleArtifactContext for alwaysEnabled beans._"""
comments: "Check what still needs to be done for this with MULE-18457 already in" [SEP] "Closing it because it was fixed by MULE-18457"

summary: "Review the MonitoredObjectStoreWrapper class's javadocs for maxEntries and entryTtl parameters."
description: """The MonitoredObjectStoreWrapper javadocs define that maxEntries should be -1 for the store to be unbounded, but actually it makes it bounded (bug). For the store to be unbounded, maxEntries should be null.  Furthermore, for the PartitionedInMemoryObjectStore the value for an unbounded store is 0, not -1, so there is an inconsistency.  This causes problems when using the ObjectStoreConnector in these settings:  a) If used in standalone mode, omitting the maxEntries parameter (null) or passing setting it to 0, causes the store to be unbounded. Setting it to -1 or any other negative value causes the store to allow 0 entries.  b) When running in cluster mode, omitting the maxEntries parameter (null) is the only way to allow for an unbounded store. Setting it to 0 allows 0 entries on the store, and setting it to -1 or any other negative number also allows 0 entries on the store.    See the method """"getMonitorablePartition"""" from the MuleObjectStoreManager class to see when scenarios a) and b) happen and how the """"settings"""" attribute is handled.    Related: https://www.mulesoft.org/jira/browse/SE-11400      You can use the app in the related ticket to test.         A similar thing happens to the entryTtl parameter."""

summary: "SFTP tests filing due to error during login in 3.8.x"
description: """Around 31 tests of SFTP transport are filing in 3.8.x branch due to an unexpected error thrown by JSch (Java Secure Channel) during login to the SFTP server.    This error could be evidenced in the following Jenkins builds:     - [Mule-3.8.x-CE-JDK8](https://muleesb.ci.cloudbees.com/job/Mule-3.8.x-CE-JDK8)   - [Mule-3.8.x-CE-JDK7](https://muleesb.ci.cloudbees.com/job/Mule-3.8.x-CE-JDK7)    After careful review of the history of the branch and a series of trials and error we were able to determine that the issue could be related to the upgrade BouncyCastle to 1.59 (---MULE-14382---: [PR|[https://github.com/mulesoft/mule/pull/6406]] )    Before this exact commit, the tests work perfectly         *Note*:     Reproducing this error locally could be quite tricky since it seems that the update to the BouncyCastle lib might be affecting the way how the host key provider file """"hostkey.ser"""" file gets generated. https://github.com/mulesoft/mule/blob/mule-3.8.x/tests/functional/src/main/java/org/mule/tck/util/sftp/SftpServer.java#L51    This file is generated on the SFTP module root path and it's not deleted during the Maven's clean lifecycle. Therefore, if you have run the test before the commit mentioned above, you won't be able to reproduce this bug unless you manually delete the file. It seems that this file does not get rewritten if it already exists.     """

summary: "Improve experience for connectors developers when provided dependencies are needed"
description: """As a good practice the connector should not expose classes from internal libraries to its API.  When a connector depends on provided dependencies that have to explicitly added as shared runtime libraries to the application, they have to use annotations to declare how the dependency should be provided by Tooling in order to be added to the pom.xml.  This information is also included in the extension model for the connector so Tooling is able to handle it. But, the extension model is also loaded before in order to be provided for Tooling. The problem is when trying to load the extension model for Tooling this dependency is missed in the plugin class loader neither the shared runtime libraries class loader.  Nowadays this is handled by connectors when writing the code, the references to these classes from the provided dependency are not referenced from classes annotation with SDK. Therefore the class not found is not thrown.  This ticket is about how we can improve this experience as it is hard for the connector's developers to do this workaround.  Different approaches:  * Include information on mule-artifact.json descriptor about the default provided dependency (has to available in remote repositories). Cases like sap or others where the jar is mavenized by users locally and included in applications (heavyweight) would be a problem  * Just keep on doing the workaround but when connector is being package try to load the extension model with the same class loader that Tooling uses (fail fast, it will not package until the extension model is loaded without the provided dependency)"""

summary: "Unable to get exception details inside mule interceptor"
description: """While executing the mule flow if exception is thrown then that exception details are not availble in the Interceptor's *last()* method. This is because the exception strategy is executed after the call to the *last()* method of the interceptor. Let's say in below flow snippet if I throw some exception then I am unable to get that exception details inside Interceptor.    <flow name=""""test-logingFlow"""">   <http:listener config-ref=""""HTTP_Listener_Configuration"""" path=""""/test/log"""" doc:name=""""HTTP""""/>   <custom-interceptor class=""""com.mule.interceptor.LoggingInterceptor"""" doc:name=""""Logging Interceptor""""/>      <!-- I want to get below exception details inside the Interceptor -->   <validation:is-not-null value=""""#[payload]"""" message=""""Payload should not be null"""" exceptionClass=""""java.lang.NullPointerException"""" doc:name=""""Null Check""""/>      <catch-exception-strategy doc:name=""""Catch Exception Strategy"""">   <set-payload value=""""Error Occured"""" doc:name=""""Set Payload""""/>   </catch-exception-strategy>   </flow>         And my LoggingInterceptor is extending AbstractEnvelopeInterceptor and the method is below.         @Override   public MuleEvent last(MuleEvent event, ProcessingTime time, long startTime, boolean exceptionWasThrown) throws MuleException \{    if (exceptionWasThrown) \{   ExceptionPayload exceptionPayload = (ExceptionPayload) event.getMessage().getExceptionPayload();   System.out.println(""""========>"""" + exceptionPayload); // it is printing null      }    } catch (Exception e) \{   LOG.error(""""Error occurred in Logging Interceptor : """", e);   }      return event;   }"""

summary: "Override Secure Properties From Anypoint Runtime Manager Properties"
description: """Replacing mule-credential-vault with secure-properties in Mule 4, made lookup properties from $\{key} to $\{secure::key}.     This update makes overriding from Anypoint Runtime Manager """"Properties"""" tab impossible, and it means we can only change the property value by rebuild/redeploy application."""
comments: "Hi Vince, can you provide more information on how to reproduce the issue?" [SEP] "Hi Pablo,    For runtimes 3.x, if you use mule-credential-vault you can access the properties by using $\{key}, similar how you access the properties you set in Runtime Manager properties tab, making the properties of mule-credential-vault overridable.     Now in 4.x you replaced mule-credentail-vault with secure-properties, and the way to access the properties in secure-properties is $\{secure::key} which is not the same way of accessing Runtime Manager properties hence it can't be overridden by runtime manager properties.     Thanks" [SEP] "[~<USER> You must set as in the mule-artifact.json secureProperties field the secure properties with the ""secure::"" prefix so CloudHub will treat them as secure.    Also, when you specify the key also add ""secure::"" prefix to the key name and it will work. I'm updating the docs as we speak.    This solution is far from ideal. We are working on making ARM discover automatically

summary: "XML SDK modules do not support reading properties from an external file"
description: """XML SDK modules do not support `<configuration-properties />`, i.e., reading properties from an external file:        is caused by     <mule:logger message=""""Creating user"""" level=""""DEBUG"""" category=""""$\{log\}"""" />    together with     <mule:configuration-properties file=""""config.properties"""" doc:name=""""Props"""" />    The exact same code (minus the `mule` XML namespace) works in a Mule app"""
comments: "They do.  It's ugly, but in order to consume a resource from a smart connector you must expose that resource to the app, as all smart connectors are macro expanded. Thus, you will use the {{<mule:configuration-properties file=""config.properties"" doc:name=""Props"" />}} in the main app, meaning that if in the main app there's no *config.properties* it fails as expected.    I have a test doing something similar with a DW script, [check out the *META-INF/mule-artifact/mule-artifact.json*|https://github.com/mulesoft-labs/smart-connectors-integration-tests/blob/master/smart-connectors/smart-connector-using-dw/src/main/resources/META-INF/mule-artifact/mule-artifact.json] file and how exposes the DWL functions to make it work (there's also an app in the same repo using that connector showing that works)          " [SEP] "After talking with [~pablo.lagreca.ce], we decided to hint users how resources should be handled for this scenarios. See MULE-14868"

summary: "JMSConnector can't reconnect if the broker breaks when the application is stopped"
description: """To reproduce the issue:    1) Deploy an application that connects succesfully to a JMS Broker (such as ActiveMQ or WebSphere) and has a reconnection strategy configured.    2) Stop the application from ARM.    3) Stop the broker.     4) Start the application.    5) Start the broker.    6) Check that the application can't reconnect through the broker is up.         Looking at the following code (    org.mule.exception.AbstractSystemExceptionStrategy#handleReconnection):              I found that if an exception ocurrs in the connection stop, the disconnection is never executed, and an invalid connection is remained. In the present scenario, as the connector is already stopped, a lifecycle exception with the following exception is triggered:    and the disconnection doesn't take place.    We can fix this issue, just guaranteeing the disconnection ocurrs indepently of the connector stop result."""

summary: "Would be great to load extension models from an static model that later can be enriched by a Mule Runtime contributions"
description: """The current approach for loading a java extension model is to use extension api loaders with enrichers and validators that will introspect the java classes and generate the model by also adding the Mule runtime needed when the extension is being used in an application execution context.  This solution works fine in Mule runtime but when extension model is needed for Tooling this adds complexity and also requires to load """"data"""" not used and needed for the extension model part that is passed to Tooling.  It would be great to either support a load strategy that doesn't set Runtime stuff (Model properties not public, or type annotations not public, either references to the class loader) as this will improve the response time when loading an extension model on Tooling.  Another approach even better would be to get the initial extension model in a persistent way (json) and that is generated without also the references to these properties published by the Mule Runtime and later this can be loaded and enriched at any point by the loader in the context of a Mule Runtime version. This also will prevent to load classes. """
comments: "We are not going to change the whole extension model loading mechanism and other approaches would be taken (cache for extension model in memory and disk)."

It may sound like a Value Provider but on this case in particular there is no need to resolve this as value provider as no need to access a remote system.    At the same time the connector developer which does the magic to correlate the parameter to something meaningful (an aggregator on this case) should also be declared this as part of the extension so the attributes definitions visitor on Mule side should know that it is referenced attribute (including scope, global element or just named element).  https://github.com/mulesoft/mule-dsl-api/blob/master/src/main/java/org/mule/runtime/dsl/api/component/AttributeDefinitionVisitor.java#L37  """

Is this not enough? " [SEP] "It would but the model property has to be propagated to be part of the Tooling API Extension Model first and second the code that process the attributes definition's from SDK has to mark the attribute as a reference (and we may need to change this API on Mule's DSL to allow define an scope, global or just named element perhaps) at least.  Let's us sync up on that to confirm this approach will work." [SEP] "This has been fixed inside the ""Stereotype"" initiaitve"

summary: "HttpRequester lifecycle is not managed properly in OAuth scenarios.  "

summary: "Non blocking operations use connector's thread to do internal mule processing"
description: """When an SDK non blocking operation finishes, the Runtime/SDK code which picks that result and performs internal Runtime logic is executed on the same thread which invoked CompletionCallback#success(Result).    So for example, when http:request obtains the response, all the internal runtime code which generates the message, handles streaming, processes the target attribute, etc, is executed on an Http selector thread instead of a runtime thread.     This has the following potential consequences:    * In connectors with few/only one selector threads (e.g: event loop) throughput might be hurt by this as the thread is performing runtime logic instead of being handling the next connection  * Custom policies, unexpected uses of the Interception API, etc, might collude with the item above and generate performance problems not detectable during our internal testing  * Memory leaks? I'm not sure if this is possible but we should review the consequences or possible failure points if an external thread is used to access objects in Mule's internal classloaders  * Security? Similar to the above, review possible impact  """
comments: "> ""internal runtime code""  This is only SDK code that creates Message/Event and processes target attribute etc, no continued chain execution will not use connector thread.    > handles streaming,  If it not ideal for this to happen on selector thread, then we should consider doing this lazily in MessageProcesaorChain instead of within SDK potentially if possible.    > uses of the Interception API  If interceptors are also executed in selector thread then this is a bug agree. I'm pretty sure they are *not* currently invoked on connector thread not but we should check and add tests.    Any hand-off to Mule thread pool will always happen after connector operation invokes non blocking callback, so it's still possible that an operation implementation can do things on the selector thread.  There are more potential possible memory/security risks from a bad connector operation implementation than from the simple creation of a MuleMessage and the evaluation of an expression on selector thread in SDK. summary: "Returned stream is closed when using pooled connections"
description: """When an operation returns an InputStream which depends on an open connection, the SDK will close that connection by default. That means that for the stream to keep open for the rest of the flow, the user must manually implement certain design patterns like:    * Manually obtaining a new connection that is bundled into a custom input stream (e.g: FTP connector)  * Use a cached connection provider which returns a client, which in turn knows how to obtain the connection (e.g: HTtp connector)    This problem has already been solved for the case of auto paging operations (e.g: db:select), we need to extrapolate the same solution to the InputStream case. Something to consider is that in the InputStream case, a corner case exists which doesn't on the auto paging one. That case is that the operation might end up returning a stream which is NOT associated with the connection (e.g: EDI Connectors which first writes to a temp file and then streams from it)"""

summary: "Race condition in reactor causes premature thread interruption resulting in empty payload with repeatable streaming with no error thrown/logged."
description: """See: https://github.com/reactor/reactor-core/issues/1107    We have seen this issue in scenarios where a flow uses:  - A scope.  - HTTP requester in the scope.    The symptom seen is that, due to premature interrupt, the HTTP response is not read and instead a Message with an empty payload is propagated in the flow. summary: "Deadlock when two JDBC connections are created concurrently before the DriverManager classloading takes place"
description: """Although this issue was originally reproduced with an scatter gather where the paths had db operations that point to different vendors (MSSQL and MySQL in the customer scenario), it could take place in any other scenario where two JDBC connections from different vendors are generated concurrently.    The deadlock only occurs if the DriverManager classloading haven't taken place.    *Steps of the deadlock:*   1) To create the VendorA JDBC connection, the org.enhydra.jdbc.standard.StandardDataSource instantiates its driver class.   2) As the DriverManager classloading hasn't taken place yet, it occurs when the driver class register itself.    3) As part of its static initialization, the DriverManager loads all the JDBC Drivers defined through the SPI mechanism. In this loading, the DriverManager treats to instantiate the Vendor B driver class.    Simulataneously the first steps occurs for generating the Vendor B JDBC Connection. As the creation of this second connection also depends on the DriverManager classloading (that is being triggered by the VendorA connection generation) it remains blocked waiting for its resolution.   On the hand, the DriverManager blocks trying to instantiate the DriverClass, whose static initialization is blocked for the cause explained in the previous paragraph.    *Notes:*   # I haven't looked at the JDK code, but for some reason, this issue doesn't take place if two or more JDBC connections of the same vendor are generated concurrently.   # Exists a [SO post|https://stackoverflow.com/questions/18640996/java-deadlock-in-classloaders] that exposes the same deadlock scenario.   # I have not found any issues reported to Oracle about this case so we should create one to ensure this can be fixed in next JDK versions.    *Verification of the deadlock and proposed solution*   I have verified the cause of the issue, removing the SPI defined in the driver jars.   The proposed solution is to guarantee the DriverManager classloading occurs before any attempt to create a JDBC Connection calling any irrelevant method of its API from our ConnectionFactory.  h2. *Deadlock Stacktrace Case 1.*     *Stacktrace Thread 1*    *Stacktrace Thread 2*    h2. *Deadlock Stacktrace Case 2.*    *Stacktrace Thread 1*    *Stacktrace Thread 2*  """
comments: "This DBPC issue seems to be related:  https://issues.apache.org/jira/browse/DBCP-272"

summary: "Expression Regex fails on detect expression when this have an unbalanced opening bracket"
description: """The following expression is not considered as a one:         \{\{#['[']}}    *(This is just a reduced expression, but happens with any expression that contains a closing bracket in some place of the expression)*    Considering the value as non-expression makes the value be considered as String, with all that this entails.    The problem is inside -> {{org.mule.runtime.core.privileged.util.TemplateParser}}    ----------    The original expression:    This is a big problem because:   # The user doesn't have a workaround.   # The user won't know why the expression doesn't get evaluated   # In this particular case is worse because of: https://www.mulesoft.org/jira/browse/MULE-14560     """
comments: "Since 3.x, there was ""support"" for nested MEL expressions which did not actually work.  It consisted of a regex that validated nested expressions but only claimed to support 6 nesting levels which was not true for 2 reasons:  1 - More than 6 levels of expressions could be nested and the full expression will still be valid (validate() == true)  2 - When parsing the expression, only 2 levels would return the correct result. After that, an error would be raised because an invalid expression was evaluated. (The expression consisted of the string that was inside the first 2 expression delimiters)    The support for nested expressions was removed. Now, anything between #[] is a valid expression, and will be parsed as is.   " [SEP] "A must have in this issue:   https://xkcd.com/1421/"

summary: "Json Schema Validator treats duplicate keys in an inconsistent way depending on the input type."
description: """When the input type for a Json Schema Validator is consumable (an InputStream or a Reader), it is being replaced with the string representation of the JsonNode object        However, the JsonNode object deletes duplicate keys by default (only the last value is conserved).   In contrast, in a non-consumable-payload scenario, the payload is not modified and all duplicate keys with its respective values remain.    In order to fix this issue we should:    # If the payload is consumable, transform it using the getPayloadAsString() method instead of the jsonNode.toString() method that modifies the original payload  # To change the default behavior and throw an exception if duplicate keys are present in the json payload to be processed.   # Introduce a system property that allows duplicate keys in json payloads.    Notes:  Although the JSON specification doesn't prohibit duplicate keys, it highly recommends to avoid them. This is a good reason to not allow them by default. In the other hand, if since any application context requirement, users pretend to allow them, they could set the system property.   By last, the specification mentions three approaches to treat duplicate keys:    # To fail indicating they are not valid.  # To ignore them.  # To mantain only the last value.     We take the (1) approach by default, and the (2) when the system property is set. The third approach causes data loss and should be avoided. """

summary: "Scheduler#scheduleAtFixedRate cancels task if triggered while previous is still running"
description: """When Scheduler#scheduleAtFixedRate is called on a Custom Scheduler with maxConcurrentTasks=1 or a pool with just 1 thread and a SynchronousQueue,         if a tasks takes longer than the rate, the next one is rejected, but then no more tasks are triggered.    Any subsequent tasks must be triggered normally after a rejection."""

summary: "Review ApplicationDescriptor's logConfigFile"
description: """Mule 3 allows users to configure a log configuration file by setting the     log.configFile property in the mule-deploy.properties file.    Mule 4 has logConfigFile as a field in the ApplicationDescriptor and there is still logic to process that value, but that field is never set and the mule-artifact.json descriptor does not have a way to configure it neither.    This tasks is to review whether or not the functionality is still required and to enable configuration of it or to remove the dead code"""

summary: "Ensure that exported resources in artifact descriptor always use linux file separator"
description: """Mule artifact descriptor (mule-artifact.json) contains the list of artifact's resources that are exported. These resource path's must be declared using Linux file separator.    As the descriptor can be manually edited, instead of just failing when the resources are not correctly written, the runtime can convert them to the expected format. """

summary: "Reduce objects allocation in order to reduce CG overhead"
description: """Based on the latests snapshots provided by the perfromance team, a good start would be MediaType and logger contexts."""

summary: "Define a mechanism to validate compatibility between extensions."
description: """For compatiblity with extensions and the runtime, we have the attribute *minMuleVersion* where the extension specifies the minimum runtime version where it's able to run successfully.    Now, we don't have any way of knowing if 2 different extensions are compatible with each other.    For example, the new Aggregators module can use the ObjectStore connector to configure some of it's attributes. If the XML definition of the ObjectStore connector changed in a future version, apps defined with aggregators old version would not work with the new ObjectStore connector"""
comments: "MTF fixes this issue"

summary: "Use a CG free thread context implementation for logging"
description: """https://logging.apache.org/log4j/2.x/manual/garbagefree.html"""

summary: "Load static REMOTELY_CLOSED_EXCEPTION"
description: """Load REMOTELY_CLOSED_EXCEPTION using service classloader"""

summary: "Use Publisher<Buffer> instead of InputStream for streaming payloads"
description: """Use Publisher<Buffer> instead of InputStream for non-blocking streaming of payloads in Mule using cpuLight threads instead of performing blocking reads using IO pool.    This depends on:  - Connector support, at a minimum HTTP.  - DW support  - Implementation of InputStream/Publisher transformers to continue support InputStream."""

summary: "Expressions containing a opening bracket are not considered as a expression"
description: """The following expression is not considered as a one:         \{\{#['[']}}    *(This is just a reduced expression, but happens with any expression that contains a closing bracket in some place of the expression)*    Considering the value as non-expression makes the value be considered as String, with all that this entails.    ----------    The original expression:    This is a big problem because:   # The user doesn't have a workaround.   # The user won't know why the expression doesn't get evaluated   # In this particular case is worse because of: https://www.mulesoft.org/jira/browse/MULE-14560     """

summary: "Not understandable exception is thrown when a transformation fails over a String with '%d'"
description: """If a transformation of a {{String}} value with a {{%d}} value fails, (like the header of DW: {{%dw 2.0}} ) makes the creation of the transformation exception fail. This could be reproduced because of: https://www.mulesoft.org/jira/browse/MULE-14561.  """

summary: "Unclear error with missing parameter in scheduler configuration"
description: """In scheduler-pools.conf commented the line for org.mule.runtime.scheduler.io.workQueue.size. After restarting the runtime it fails to start successfully with an unclear error message. Adding -Dmule.verbose.exceptions=true will point that the error is related to scheduler configuration.    The expected result should be that there is an error indicating which parameter is invalid or undefined."""

summary: "When a Service/CoreExtension start fails, not started objects are tried to stop"
description: """Say there are 2 services, A and B to be started (code in MuleServiceManager). A start fails, then MuleServiceManager tries to stop both A and B. Since B hasn't been started, ith shouldn't be stopped.    This may also be happening with Core Extensions and Server Plugins"""

summary: "Add Icons in all the extensions"
description: """None of the actual connectors packages their connectors inside the mule plugin, start doing this."""

summary: "When hot deploying an invalid domain configuration, DeploymentService is answering corrupt MuleApplications"
description: """Steps to reproduce (using Studio, which polls the agent on the /mule/applications endpoint):   # Create an application and a domain, link the application to the domain (in project preferences)   # Run the application   # Once everything is deployed, make a change in the domain configuration XML which would result in a parsing error (this will get copied to the running application in mule)     * On the console, the following exception will appear (repeated every 200ms, due to the polling from Studio on the agent)      Apparently, the exception would originate from the DeploymentService returning an Application object with a null domain under these circumstances."""
comments: "When a domain is redeployed, all the applications are undeployed and deployed again once the domain was successfully deployed.  When the app is undeployed, many properties in the Application instance will be nullified (classlaoder, registry, etc) and there will be no domain because that instance is being obtained from a DomainRepository that only contains successfully deployed domains.    I will check if this can be changed without introducing additional problems, but in the meantime, there is a easy change that can be done on the agent side. On this line https://github.com/mulesoft/mule-agent-plugin/blob/9a26844d777d029c98f1b0ace5a5bb77e157b088/mule-agent/mule-agent-services/mule-agent-application-service/src/main/java/com/mulesoft/agent/services/application/AgentApplicationService.java#L792 instead of getting the domain name from the domain artifact, just get it from the application descriptor." [SEP] "This has been fixed on the agent: https://github.com/mulesoft/mule-agent-plugin/commit/62c47b933ecb3e17db

summary: "CXF is writing payload after the latest boundary in multipart/related scenarios "
description: """In multipart/related scenarios, CXF writes the payload after the last boundary.  This only happens with stream and string payloads which are writing as objects by the OutputPayloadInterceptor instead of using an XMLStreamWriterCallback and flushes the payload immediately as happens with XMLStreamReader payloads.     Additionally, Content-id headers that are commonly used to identify each body part, should be taken as the name of the attachment, if the name of the part is not present in the content-type or the content-disposition headers."""

summary: "XMLSecureFactories is setting unsupported properties at factories creation."
description: """Differents customers are seing the following warning log when the XML Schema Validation Filter is used:    {code:xml}  """"Can't configure XML entity expansion for %s (%s), this could introduce XXE and BL vulnerabilities""""  {code}    which makes them feel unsafe and confused.  This log was introduced in order to warning about the usage of some old JAXP implementations that don't support the last features required by the JDK specification (for instance, the Apache Xerces endorsed libraries that were removed in 3.9.0).   However, this warning is present in 3.9.x as well where we are using the JAXP implementation provided by the JDK.   After some investigation, I note that in some factories, wrong properties are being set, probably by a typo causing exceptions like:      {code:java}  """"org.xml.sax.SAXNotRecognizedException: Property http://javax.xml.XMLConstants/property/accessExternalStylesheet is not recognized""""  {code}    We should fix it taking into account what properties are supported for each factory."""

summary: "Allows custom schedulers to use a `runInCaller` rejection policy"
description: """When the http requester receives data, it dispatches the work to cpuLite to continue exectuting. If the cpuLite pool is configured for a SynchronousQueue, when the cpuLite is full the work will be rejected mid-flow.    In order to avoid this, the scheduler should enable for the http requester selector to use a `runInCaller` policy, so the flow continues executing in the requester thread."""

summary: "Mule 4 - Flow representation model"
description: """This epic is to define a model to represent a flow and flows relationships. The idea of this model is that it can be used for having different representations of the flows.    The model may have different layers of data that can be applied to. So we can generate graphical representations of the flow with more/less data when needed. For instance, there may be a layer for *monitoring* that shows TPS, error rate, latency, etc. Another layer that shows main configuration values for each operation to provide more context to the user, another layer that shows execution order of operations, etc.    The model is meant to be reused by every product that must create a graphical representation of a flow."""

summary: "Spike: Define proper logging infrastructure to use in the runtime"
description: """This is to define the proper mechanism for logging so we can enable/disable logging per feature instead of per class and provide also the possible options for logging in the artifact configuration specification.    It must support requirements from the monitoring document"""

summary: "Add notifications for outbound and inbound data from external services"
description: """We need to provide notifications support OOTB for inbound and outbound operations. They must be typified and proper data related to the operation must be provided so they can be use for monitoring of the application.    This is just to implement the infrastructure so extensions can support this kind of notifications. Work on each connector must be done separately."""

summary: "Spike on supporting runtime configuration changes"
description: """Define how runtime configuration changes can be applied for every configuration part of the artifact."""

summary: "Spike on supporting deployment time configuration"
description: """See how deployment time configuration can be applied in a generic way to the artifact configuration so that it can be applied for any configuration part of the artifcat"""

summary: "Define the artifacts parts that must be configurable"
description: """We need to define the different parts of the application that should be configurable by the user including those that can be modified at runtime at those that can only be configured at deployment time. Of course everything that can be configured at runtime MUST be configurable at deployment time.    For instance:  Runtime configuration:  * Log level  * Logging categories    Deployment configuration:  * Placeholders  * Certificates  * Timeouts  * Connectors connection provider configuration attributes  """

summary: "Define the specification for defining configuration options for artifacts"
description: """A user, before being able to define the configuration of an artifact, it must be able to know which are the things that can be configure and get some context about it. Many times it's the devops task to actually manage and deploy artifacts on behalf of users.    The idea is to create an AMF vocabulary and define the whole specification for defining the configurable parts of an application.    This will be the source of the platform to show the user the possible configuration options. It must be generic enough so new configuration options can be added in the future."""

summary: "Mule 4 - Artifacts Configuration"
description: """This epic is for task related to artifact configuration. It may be deployment time configuration or runtime time configuration.     The idea is that both are configured in exactly the same way. The only difference is that not everything can be configured at runtime.    This document must be used as base for this epic: [https://docs.google.com/document/d/1_pnJZpCTjAQLBQUF4hwdfFw3k_7e0nu1HZMyal-SN6I/edit|http://example.com]"""

summary: "When creating ApplicationModel and ComponentModels inner beans should have a name so they can be later retrieved"

At the same time the org.mule.runtime.config.internal.BeanDependencyResolver had to be patched to work on this case as whenever the code to resolve dependencies from ApplicationModel was also returning as bean name the aggregatorName (needed for LazyInit) but the aggregator which is a source is created as inner child bean with Spring therefore the name declared on the aggregator is not the name that goes to the Spring bean and it couldn't be retrieved later in order to set as dependency."""

summary: "Improve multipart support considering parts mime type and iteration use case"
description: """DataWeave provides access to multipart content allowing access to parts by name or index. However, we have not optimise access for iteration use cases. Trying to access all parts will cause the inferred mime type to be multipart and fail while setting java as output will alter the internal data to be java as well.  We should either create a java object to represent the parts in a way that keeps their mime type or provide a better way to achieve that use case. In any case, we need to make sure that the content access always takes the mime type in the parts headers into consideration.    ====    Use cases that should work:  * As a user I'd like to save some files received in an HTTP request (HTTP Listener - For Each - File Write)  * As a user I'd like to send an email using certain files received in an HTTP request (HTTP Listener - Email)  * As a user I'd like to send some Email attachments to an FTP server (Email - FTP)"""

summary: "Spike - Resolve dependencies between components on ApplicationModel using ExtensionModel instead of ComponentBuildingDefinitionProvider "
description: """The way MinimalApplication is generated for Tooling is by resolving the graph of dependencies for a componentModel with the ComponentBuildingDefinitionProviders. It would be nice to use the ExtensionModels so we don't need the parsers mechanism at this point and we can use this feature in other context such DataSense.    In addition to that, there are cases already hardcoded on MinimalApplication such flow-ref, global-error-model or aggregators that should be handled by this mechanism where the attribute is a soft reference (SDK and ExtensionModel could provide this information but at the ComponentBuildingDefinitionProvider should not be declared as reference. The way flow-ref and aggregators handle this is internally the components once created look for the referenced component by the given name."""

Therefore, MVEL gets the method from the previous return type class, but invokes it by reflection passing the current object. The following exception is triggered:        """

summary: "Error handler of operation policies can't handle invalid requests"
description: """There is no possible way to handle invalid requests from the error handler of the operation policy.   An invalid request would be, for example, making a request to an inexistent resource (500 server error), or receiving a 403 from the server.  After executing the 'execute-next' operator, if the request is invalid, the operation result is catched by the application error handler instead of using the policy one.    This test reproduces the problem: https://github.com/mulesoft/mule-ee/commit/9ba58d92f083f26f061f544a0a69f3c7ef9b2f4b"""

summary: "Expression error when accessing Json payload created by set-payload"
description: """Having the following configuration      produces the following error:    org.mule.runtime.core.internal.exception.MessagingException: """"Java Object Source does not support Input Stream"""" evaluating expression: """"payload.value"""".  ; ErrorType: MULE:EXPRESSION  Caused by: org.mule.runtime.core.api.expression.ExpressionRuntimeException: """"Java Object Source does not support Input Stream"""" evaluating expression: """"payload.value"""".  Caused by: org.mule.runtime.api.el.ExpressionExecutionException: Java Object Source does not support Input Stream    Workaround:     Set the mimeType using DW instead of the set-payload attribute      """
comments: "The workaround is not actually a workaround but the way you are supposed to use set-payload.   The mimeType attribute is an advance feature to set the mimeType of the result of the expression when DW does not know how to handle it. For cases where DW knows how to process the required mimeType, set it inside the expression."

summary: "Refactor aggregators to be cluster aware"
description: """All aggregators should work in a cluster.    That implies processing each event only once, regarding the node the flow is being executed and making sure that if a node is disconnected, all the information that was handled by that node, was properly persisted and shared with the other ones to continue as if there was no error."""

summary: "Allow aggregators to be configured with a privateObjectStore"
description: """Since all aggregators should be able to be configured with a custom objectStore, we should be able to do it defining a private object store within the aggregator it self.     Check the way the idempotent-message-validator can be configured. """

summary: "Add support for configurable custom objectStore"
description: """All the aggregators should persist all information in an objectStore so that the application works if distributed in a cluster or it suffers from an error that causes it to shutdown.  The objectStore should have the possibility to be configured by the user. """

summary: "Add AggregatorAttributes support"
description: """Add AggregatorAttributes support when executing a route within an aggregator.   Make sure the attributes can be reached from a DW expression. """

summary: "Add errors for aggregator module"
description: """Define ErrorTypes and ErrorProviders for the different exceptions that can happen in the aggregators module according to the spec."""

summary: "Implement basic group-based-aggregator functionality with SDK"
description: """The aggregators module should have a group-based-aggregator which can aggregate events by groupId.  The aggregator should be able to be configured to get the groupId with an expression.    Also, consider the configuration for group eviction"""

summary: "Implement basic time-based-aggregator functionality with SDK"
description: """The aggregators module should have a time-based-aggregator which can aggregate events until period is completed.   If that happens, a registered listener should be notified.   Also, consider a maxSize for syncronous group release"""

summary: "Implement basic size-based-aggregator functionality with SDK"
description: """The aggregators module should have a size-based-aggregator which can aggregate events until the amount reaches an expected size.   If that happens, both a listener and the on-complete route should be executed.  Also, consider a timeout for the group to be released asynchronously"""

summary: "All issues related to the new Aggregator module"
description: """Epic referring to all issues related to the Aggregators module.    The spec for the module:  https://docs.google.com/document/d/1pQSiOb0QJc4NVMUt_lhvM3Yv6RZoPFHzsanmB29pnaw/edit#heading=h.uctx9os4aypn"""

Putting a breakpoint on the LoggerMessageProcessor shows that the logger is being executed the whole 3 times, instead of only once.      """

summary: "Add support for notifications that must be able to consume managed streams"
description: """We need a mechanism for the notifications listeners to work with managed cursor streams.     Today a notification that consumes a managed stream may fail if the stream was closed because the flow finished before the notification execution. We need a mechanism to properly handle those cases."""
comments: "One possible solutions is to allow notifications to declare if they may access the managed streams within the Event.   - If they declare not to use them then we can proxy the managed streams and fail as soon as they try to use it with a clear error message. This will make very clear the problem they have, how to fix it and will happen as soon as they try out their notification   - If the declare to use it then we need to have a mechanism for the notification to inform that is done so the managed stream can be properly closed"

summary: "Only support back-preasure configuration in HTTP"
description: """Until we clearly define an implementation for each of the back preasure strategies, we should only support WAIT as an option in all sources. Having a default implementation for other strategies make no sense since each of them require proper treatment and if we don't do that then we would be breaking backwards compatibility in the future"""
comments: "Agree we shouldn't rush to expose these strategies in general until we are clear on how each connector should implement them, especially for FAIL. This is not the case however with DROP for JMS/AMQP/VM though as there is only one possible implementation here; drop and ack anyway. "

summary: "Serializable classes do not define serialVersionUID"
description: """Several classes that implements Serializable do not define a serialVersionUID.    As a result due to changes in classes of 4.1.0 (1.1.0 of the API artifacts) old artifacts such as MUnit 2.0.0 no longer works with 4.1.0/1.1.0 artifacts for they were built agains the classes in 4.0.0/1.0.0.         The solution serialVersionUID generated by the JVM for those classes in 4.0.0/1.0.0 and specify it in the serializable classes of 4.1.0/1.1.0.     In particular MUnit is failing due to changes in the *class org.mule.runtime.module.embedded.internal.DefaultDeploymentConfigurationBuilder.DeploymentConfigurationImpl*          """

summary: "Preserve Event streams for async notification listeners"
description: """A number of notifications carry an Event in them for listeners to consume. However, if the notification is async then the listener may consume it once the Event has completed, meaning any streams in it will be closed.  We should generate an Event copy in that case with an EventContext that's a child of the original one. Then, the notification dispatch to all listeners would be part of what makes the Event complete and streams would remain open."""

summary: "Create with-lock-key scope to support update scenarios"
description: """Today, to update the contents of an Object Store entry you need to first obtain it, modify it and then store it updated. In between, other updates might take place or it may be read with the outdated contents meaning you have no guaranteed the data will be consistent. In order to guarantee no other reads/writes are performed the key must be locked before all operations execute and released after, even in case of failures."""

summary: "Plugin bean factories are being loaded with the application classloader."
description: """This issue was found in the CXF Module (part of the compatibility plugin) in which the JDK implementation of JAXB is being loaded instead of the implementation given through the maven dependency.     For creating a JAXB Context, the ContextFinder class (present in the JDK), looks for defined SPI in the classloader, and if not present, instantiates the default JDK factory. Currently, as the application classloader is being used, the SPI provided by the JAXB implementation that we use is not being found.     Looking at the code, I found that the classloader change should be applied [in the following method|https://github.com/mulesoft/mule/blob/mule-4.x/modules/spring-config/src/main/java/org/mule/runtime/config/internal/dsl/spring/ObjectFactoryClassRepository.java#L83] in which the object factories are instantiated.          """

summary: "Fix NPE in CXF module."
description: """The folloxing part of the code:    in the [MuleHeadersInInterceptor|https://github.com/mulesoft/mule-compatibility-plugin/blob/master/modules/cxf/src/main/java/org/mule/compatibility/module/cxf/support/MuleHeadersInInterceptor.java#L98] class is causing NPE when the evaluation of ternary operator gives true.    This happens because a null value is given as input to a function that receives an int (and native types can't handle this value)."""

summary: "Add the ability to specify the data folder location for an application"
description: """In Mule 3 it was possible to deploy an updated version of an application using the exact folder/zip name of a previous one in order to reuse the temp data folder.   In Mule 4 since the data folder gets created based on the GAV of the artifact (without consider patch version) then it's no possible to do this unless the updated application GAV is the same one as the previous app or only differs on the patch version which it would be just a hack to make it work.    We need to properly define a mechanism so an updated version of an application that changes the major or minor version, can reuse the same data folder."""
comments: "I agree completely with having this feature since it allows for different versions of the same API to exists in the same RT instance, which is a great feature.    However, this should not come at the expense of complicating the most simple use case which is upgrading an app to its next minor version. If v1.0.0 of my is using persistent ObjectStore or VM queues to persist non API objects, next version of the same app should still be able to deserialize those objects.     Plus, for the cases in which two different versions of the same app is deployed at the same time, we also need to determine whether we allow or not for the two apps to use the same storage. Functionally it makes sense, but we'll probably run into race conditions everywhere. (I'm totally fine with this last paragraph being tackled in a separate ER, up to you)" [SEP] "We also need to consider the gateway app scenario in which the same gateway app with different configuration (but same GAV) can be deployed multiple times in the runtime." [SEP] "

summary: "Remove patching scenarios"
description: """With the inclusion of deployment of patched artifacts on mule 4.0.0, some inconsistencies were introduced in the way artifacts are identified and how deployment notifications are sent.    Artifacts are identified by the file/folder name, so when a patch for app1.0.0 is deployed, it will be installed in app-1.0.1 and the identifier for the patched app will be app-1.0.1. When the patch is deployed, the old one must be undeployed, so this must send undeployment notifications. These notifications will use the app-1.0.0 ID. This makes hard to correlate all the notifications send on this deployment. (and in particular, it makes hard to implement MULE-14118 in a consistent way).    The deployment must manage the patching scenarios (on both the folder and artifact ID) in a similar way to the used to manage the artifact data folder (see DeployableArtifactDescriptor#getDataFolderName), that consist of removing the bug fix version  from the folder/ID.     Additionally, as the full version is not part of the ID, it could make sense to include a new field on the deployment notifications to provide this value"""
comments: "There were multiple things that didn't make sense finally:   - Change the folder and artifact name based on the GAV since it prevents mutiple apps with the same GAV from being deployed and this is a MUST since we support deployment of apps customized like we do for gateways in 3.x   - Support patching based on the GAV. This is a management concern. It's better to have the rules for patching in ARM.   - Reuse of the data folder. This can be done by just causing the runtime to do a redeployment if we don't consider the GAV."

summary: "SDK communicates wrong expected mime type to EL "
description: """When a parameter is resolved via the expression language, the expected data type is used to indicate how it should instantiate the result. For example, HTTP might need a Multimap<String, String> so the expression language will know it needs to turn all values to Strings and consider all key values. Those expected data types always carry the ANY mime type instead of """"application/java"""" and are therefore useless to optimise the transformations (if the expected data type matches the current one no extra transformations are needed)."""

summary: "Provide mechanism to trace a request as it goes through the policies chain."
description: """It would be a great troubleshooting tool to have a logging level that once enabled allows to view the whole trip of a request as it goes through. For each request we would expect to see:   # Each policy it goes through. In source and operation.   # Outcome of each policy (allow or reject and reason)   ## It could be an extra to have enhanced logging on specific API processing. Step by step and outcome with details   # Final response    Each log message should be accompanied by a request ID in order to be able to trace a specific request as it goes through the policies chain in concurrency scenarios and it should log all information of the event, except for payload (AGW-1926 was created for payload logging).    This feature will allow us and our customers to troubleshoot what happens to a specific request. For example which policy is rejecting it and for which reason. Or to tell which policies have been applied to it and assert that was the original intention, just to name a couples examples."""

summary: "MessagePropertyMapContext isn't deserializable with Kryo"
description: """We need to do a deep copy of an _org.mule.el.context.MessagePropertyMapContext_ object therefore we're serializing and deserializing the object with Mule's ObjectSerializer:  {code:java}  muleContext.getObjectSerializer().deserialize(muleContext.getObjectSerializer().serialize(map), muleContext.getExecutionClassLoader());  {code}  where _map_ is a MessagePropertyMapContext set from MEL (_#[message.outboundProperties]_). However, with Kryo, deserialization fails:        _NullPointerException_ is thrown because the MuleMessage is missing from the deserialised _MessagePropertyMapContext_ object. _MessagePropertyMapContext_ is a core class and it doesn't make sense in our opinion that it cannot be deserialized."""

summary: "Review graceful shutdown with <async/> scope"
description: """This was found when trying to fix flaky test:    Apparently, since the flow has an <async/> scope, the execution is done quickly and it starts stoping and disposing components prior to the initialization of other components by different threads.     Specifically, a ConfigurationProviderToolingAdapter was created. That class initializes a LifecycleAwareConfigurationInstance containing a retryScheduler.    Sometimes, when the configurationProvider was being registered in the registry, the registry was already being stopped or disposed by the main thread so when the test ended, the retryScheduler was still alive.    The problem with the test was that isGracefulShutdown() was false, but we still need to review if there is no other case where a problem like this could happen. (e.g: With a small timeout)."""
comments: "The graceful shutdown seems to be working properly.       The problem found was that the registration of a new object in the registry was not synchronized. So, there was a possible case where while the muleContext was stoping/disposing, and consequently the registry and all the objects held by it, a new object was being registered from another thread.     So, when the registry was asked for the Stoppable/Disposable objects to stop/dispose, there was none, and after the stop/dispose phase, this new object (Stoppable/Disposable) was registered. This led to the object never being stopped/disposed."

summary: "Add Support for HTTP response timeouts lower than 500 ms"
description: """Due to the Grizzly Implementation, the responseTimeout attribute in the HTTP Requester only works as expected with a value upper or equal than 500 ms. Lower values cause a random behavior.    [Grizzly implementation|https://github.com/javaee/grizzly-ahc/blob/master/src/main/java/com/ning/http/client/providers/grizzly/GrizzlyAsyncHttpProvider.java#L244] defines a interval to check the timeout expiration using the global request timeout (defined at client level). The default value for this interval is 500ms, and if the timeout is lower than it, the interval is set to (timeout - 10). If the timeout is lower than 10ms,  this is ignored, and the checking interval is directly set to 1 second.    As we don't define the requestTimeout at client level but at request level, Grizzly calculates the checking interval using the default global request timeout that is 1 minute. Therefore, the checking interval is always 500 ms, so we can only guarantee an expected behavior with a timeout lower or equal than this.    Possible approaches to face this ER are:   # To modify the grizzly implementation, and redefining the checking interval by request (this approach seems to not be trivial).    # To allow to configure a global request timeout in the HTTP Requester and to indicate in the documetation that if a timeout lower than 500 ms is expected, the global timeout must be also set, giving the above explanation.     I have tested the second approach, with 20 ms of timeout, and it works consistently.     """

The main use case for this is to create operations/sources which return/receive non java types loaded from a schema, which is required to be available at design time. description: """*XML Types doesn't work OOTB in DB connector*   * XML is a valid JBCD type, but trying to use one of this to insert a value using the DB connector won't work out of the box  due to the auto coercion of DW.   * If the payload is:    And use it in DB:    This will fail because the {{payload}} value will be transformed automatically to a Java Map.  * A workaround from the Mule Developer side is to use the {{write(payload, 'application/xml')}} to prevent the coercion to Java.  * A, no so good, fix from the connector side is to receive the {{inputParameters}} parameter as a {{Map<String, TypedValue<Object>>}}, but as consequense only references can be done from the {{inputParameters}} transformation. (This is a limitation of DW)    Review which is the best scenario, the current one or a possible fix from the connector side, or talk with Mariano Achaval, if a better solution can be done from DW side.    In case that the resolution is to maintain the current behaviour document this workaround to make it work for XML types."""

summary: "Introduce the concept of Path location classification"
description: """The PathModel needs to be enhanced so that parameters representing a file path can have more information regarding where those paths are going to point to. This will be helpful for tooling.    A location attribute will be added to the PathModel. This attribute is an enum:   * EMBEDDED: The path points to a resource inside the mule app. UI should render this as a file picker   * EXTERNAL: The path points to a resource outside of the mule application. Most likely (but not necessarily) in the file system or an external URL. No file picker, just a regular text box   * ANY: Resource can point to any of the above. A file picker can be offered, but the user needs to be able to also specify any random path"""

summary: "DataWeave Java written functions doesn't work correctly with non-java types"
description: """*DataWeave Java written Functions doesn't work with non-java types*    * DataWeave java written functions always coerces the input parameters to Java Types, this makes impossible to use these functions with non-Java types.  * Take the following function written with the SDK as example: {code}      public InputStream validateXml(InputStream payload) {      aValidation(payload);      return payload;    }{code}   This have two main issues:  ** All the parameters are coerced to Java, so if i call the function with the following XML: {code:xml}<?xml version=""""1.0"""" encoding=""""UTF-8""""?>  <?xml-stylesheet type=""""text/css"""" href=""""cd_catalog.css""""?>  <CATALOG>    <CD>      <TITLE>Empire Burlesque</TITLE>      <ARTIST>Bob Dylan</ARTIST>      <COUNTRY>USA</COUNTRY>      <COMPANY>Columbia</COMPANY>      <PRICE>10.90</PRICE>      <YEAR>1985</YEAR>    </CD>  </CATALOG>{code} this value will be automatically coerced to a Java object which can't be injected into the function which expects an InputStream:  {code}org.mule.weave.v2.module.core.exception.UnexpectedFunctionCallTypesException: 1| Fn::validateXml(payload)     ^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^  You called the function 'logger' with these arguments:     1: Object ({CATALOG: {CD: {TITLE: """"Empire Burlesque"""", ARTIST: """"Bob Dylan"""", COUNTRY: """"USA...)    But it expects arguments of these types:    1: Binary      Trace:    at 'logger' in (anonymous:1:1)    at 'main'   in (anonymous:1:1)  {code}  ** Coertion to Java loose data: If you now have the following XML : {code:xml}<?xml version=""""1.0"""" encoding=""""UTF-8""""?>  <?xml-stylesheet type=""""text/css"""" href=""""cd_catalog.css""""?>  <CATALOG>    <CD>      <TITLE>Empire Burlesque</TITLE>      <ARTIST>Bob Dylan</ARTIST>      <COUNTRY>USA</COUNTRY>      <COMPANY>Columbia</COMPANY>      <PRICE>10.90</PRICE>      <YEAR>1985</YEAR>    </CD>    <CD>      <TITLE>Hide your heart</TITLE>      <ARTIST>Bonnie Tyler</ARTIST>      <COUNTRY>UK</COUNTRY>      <COMPANY>CBS Records</COMPANY>      <PRICE>9.90</PRICE>      <YEAR>1988</YEAR>    </CD>  </CATALOG>{code} the automatic coertion will return you : {code}{CATALOG={CD={TITLE=Hide your heart, ARTIST=Bonnie Tyler, COUNTRY=UK, COMPANY=CBS Records, PRICE=9.90, YEAR=1988}}}{code}. And because Java maps doesn't support repeated keys, which XML does, you loose data, in addition to XML element attributes that also are not mapped to java automatically.    As conclusion, java written DataWeave functions behave totally different than any other DW function, they are not consistent with any native DW functions, and an example of this is the *lookup* function, ({{lookup(flowName, newPayload}}), a user will think that *newPayload* will be a plain injection of the given value, but not, the *flowName* flow will be executed with a coerced value of the *newPayload* expression, with all the consequences explained above.     Functions as {{xpath3}}, json and xml module related functions are not possible with the current behaviour. """

"""When using a SynchronousQueue on an Executor, even if calling prestartAllCoreThreads(), a race condition may occur where the worker threads are started but before it starts to take elements from the queue, the user of the Executor dispatches some task to it.

summary: "Make schedulers monitor system load and reject tasks if resources are tight"
description: """Schedulers should start rejecting work when resources start getting tight.    An approach to do this would be to have a global inflight event counter, working in a similar fashion to the maxConcurrency at the flow level, and trigger backpressure if that value is exceeded.    The limit would in turn be adjustable by monitoring resources usage (memory primarily), increasing the limit if resources are idle, or decreasing if most are busy. The ultimate goal of this is to operate at max capacity but triggering backpressure before throwing an OOM."""
comments: "This could prevent errors like the one in EE-6862, since the source would not trigger the flow when memory was scarce, preventing an OOM that would render the runtime useless."

summary: "MVEL functions with object instantiations doesn't work well in heavy load."
description: """When a defined mvel function that instantiates an object in every call, is used in heavy load, mvel is showing an unpredictable behavior. This is caused because the same [NewObjectNode|https://github.com/mulesoft/mvel/blob/master/src/main/java/org/mule/mvel2/ast/NewObjectNode.java], that is not thread safe, is used in multiple and concurrent invocations without synchronization.   Possible solutions to this issue are:    - Synchronize the getReducedValueAccelerated method but it would probably cause an important performance degradation.  - Make this class thread safe. However this class wasn't designed as thread safe from the beginning and the refactor would be considerable.    - In each invocation of the function, to work with a copy of the NewObjectNode. This seems to be the better solution to apply.     Update:  - After running performance tests, I found out that synchronizing the getReducedValueAccelerated only for initializate the NewObjectNode status, seems to be the best option. In heavy load, the first thread will be the resposible to the initialization."""

summary: "Allow correlationID to be customised in source using a value from the incoming message."
description: """By default, the flow should use either an incoming external correlationID or a UUID as a correlationID for a new Flow execution but there are also use cases where:     - The user would prefer to define there own method to generate a correlationID (e.g. use a simple timestamp instead of Mule's UUID)   - The user would prefer to use a natural correlationID from the message payload (e.g. a unique order Id)     This later use case was possible in Mule 3 via the message-info-mapping functionality which has been removed in Mule 4. The Mule 3 approach had issues though, particularly when combined with the fact that correlationId could be mutated during the flow. In Mule 4 the correlationID should be allowed to optionally be defined in the source but then fixed throughout the whole flow execution."""
comments: "This support has been implemented on https://www.mulesoft.org/jira/browse/MULE-13927"

summary: "Log correlationId as part of HTTP requester selector log output"
description: """Mule now consistently logs the event correlationId as part of per-message log output.  Log output produced by the selector thread in the requester including wire logging does not, however, include this correlationId making the correlation of wire logging output and other log output extremely hard.    While the use of a custom header has been suggested as an option to help here, this is not really suitable because:  - This would need to be explicitly configured by the user, and therefore is of no use from a support perspective.  - Even if the correlationID is set manually by the user in all new apps, the use of this value by grep/splunk and other tools is still a significant issue, because it is not possible to treat all log entries in the same way; in some the correlationId would be matched in the header of the log output, and in others the content of the log output would need to be searched.    This would need to be a point solution for HTTP for sure, but that is because this is specific to HTTP and there is no way of implementing anything like this generically given each connector has it's own specific implementations.    While this involves some amount of customization in the use of AHC in the HTTP connector the value gained from this improvement outweighs the cost of doing this pretty significantly IMO especially given the wide usage of HTTP."""
comments: "This PR could address this ticket: https://github.com/mulesoft/mule-http-service/pull/36" [SEP] "See linked issue"

summary: "CorrelationId should be propagated from flow to other flows or systems via connector operations"
description: """When a JMS/AMQP or other messaging connector operation is used (e.g. jms:publish) the Flow correlationID should be propagated via the outbound message as an external correlationID (as defined in the relevant specification).    This is important both from a functional perspective to allow for traceability of transactions across multiple flows and external applications via analytics/insights, log output and customer use of Splunk and other such tools. But is also very important for the supportability of customer solutions by our support team.    Given traceability and supportability is a cross-cutting concern of huge value to users of our platform, propagation of correlationID's for this purpose should happen by default and the user should not need to i) turn it on specifically ii) have any specific knowledge of what specific headers should be configured manually in order to achieve the necessary propagation to achieve traceability and supportability.    This applies to all connectors where the concept of correlationId is present in the spec, but we should seriously consider also using """"X-Correlation-ID"""" on outbound HTTP messages too. (this is not a standard but is best practice). See: http://hilton.org.uk/blog/microservices-correlation-id https://blog.logentries.com/2016/12/the-value-of-correlation-ids/ See: MULE-14085     We already allow for the correlationID to be customised using a DW expression (in some connectors) but the default behaviour, if this is not configured, should be to propagate the flow correlationID.    Note that to achieve traceability/supportability and functionally match Mule 3 in this regard MULE-13927 is also needed.    A future improvement, if there is need for it, would be to not only allow the outbound correlationId to be customized, but to allow it's use to be disabled completely. See MULE-14086."""
comments: "This behaviour shouldn't be default. It is trivial to propagate the correlation id with DW, and to have consistent behavior across connectors, that should be the way to do it.    Maybe there should be a way to enforce this behavior generally (for bridges and so) vis system property or an entry in the application descriptor?" [SEP] "[~rodrigo.merino]   - The fact that it is trivial to manually set is not relevant.  It is important that the correlationId is propagated by default (for the reasons detailed above) without explicit user configured, regardless of how trivial/complex this configuration is to configure manually.  - I don't understand your consistency concern. Why would it be inconsistent for JMS, which supports the concept of correlation, to propagate a correlationId, and for TCP not to? That's simply an expected difference between connectors due to the differing nature of the underlying messaging system, not an inconsistency.  - The idea of changing this behaviour via a flag or system property for s

summary: "CorrelationId is not propagated from connector sources to Flow"
description: """When a JMS/AMQP or other messaging connector source is used the inbound correlationID (as defined in the relevant specification) should be propagated to the Flow by creating the EventContext using this external correlationID instead of passing null and allowing it to generate a UUID.    This is important because:  - It is a regression from 3.x  - The EventContext javadoc states that external correlationId is available via getCorrelationId()  - This was discussed in detail with use case and reviewed by support when new eventContext/event/message model was defined and current implementation does not match what was defined.  - From a user perspective, it is important that if an external correlationId is present that this is seen in log entries and in insights/analytics records instead of a UUID in the platform.  - From a supportability perspective not maintaining the external correlationID makes it much harder for users and our support team to find/resolve issues.    This applies to all connectors where the concept of correlationId is present in the spec, but we should also consider using """"X-Correlation-ID"""" if present in inbound HTTP messages too.  (this is not a standard but is best practice)    A future improvement should allow the correlationID to be customised in the source using a DW expression DW, but the default behaviour should be to use an external correlationID. See MULE-13930.      """

summary: "DeployableMuleContextListener doesn't work in embedded applications scenarios."
description: """When a Mule standalone instance is embedded in a Tomcat Server and shared among many mule applications, SpringRegistryBootstrap bean (that is responsible for loading classes from registry-bootstrap.properties) is not being loaded as a configuration resource of the application, causing DI issues.  Probably this issue was introduced after a refactor in [SpringXmlConfigurationBuilder|https://github.com/mulesoft/mule/blob/mule-3.x/modules/spring-config/src/main/java/org/mule/config/spring/SpringXmlConfigurationBuilder.java] class, in which the SpringRegistryBootstrap bean was moved from mule-spring-config.xml to its own file.  Our test cases didn't break because they are using the [MuleXmlBuilderContextListener|https://github.com/mulesoft/mule/blob/mule-3.x/modules/builders/src/main/java/org/mule/config/builders/MuleXmlBuilderContextListener.java] that creates a mule standalone instance per app, instead of using the  [DeployableMuleXmlContextListener|https://github.com/mulesoft/mule/blob/mule-3.x/modules/builders/src/main/java/org/mule/config/builders/DeployableMuleXmlContextListener.java] that searches a preexisting mule instance configured and started at server level.    Update:  After some investigation, apparently, the DeployableMuleXmlContextListener never worked.   I could find several issues, reported a time ago, in which DI or lifecycle problems are described.  Lifecycle problems are caused because mule context is started in MuleTomcatListener, defined at server level which shares the same mule context statically among all application that defines a DeployableMuleXmlContextListener in its web.xml. Then, when each application wants to load its registry bootrstrap, there are lifecycle inconsistences since mule context has been started but all the application beans still are not initiliazed.  This seems to be a design flaw, that will be removed altogheter or fixed for future versions.    """

summary: "Begin a TX inside of another should make the app fail instead of commit the original TX"
description: """With the current Mule Config:    *Current Behaviour*:   When trying to execute the second *try* scope, the original TX will be committed, as consequence the *operations1* will be executed inside the first TX, *operations2* will be executed in the second TX, and *operations3* which statically should be executed inside the first TX, it will be executed outside of any TX because the corresponded TX is already closed.    *Expected Behaviour* :   If a TX is tried to be opened inside other TX this should fail.    *Things to consider*:   * *ALWAYS_BEGIN* loses any kind of sense to keep existing, because in any case that you force to create a new TX, or it will be created because no previously was created this means the same behaviour as *BEGIN_OR_JOIN*.   * The current behaviour of the *ALWAYS_BEGIN* as the JavaDocs say is the equivalent of the J2EE's *RequiresNew*, but the expected behaviour if the *RequiresNew* is totally different when a new one is required, the original TX is suspended for the execution of the new one, behaviour that doesn't occur in Mule.   * If *ALWAYS_BEGIN* keeps existing, and this construction is invalid, this could be validated in a future when """"compiling"""" the application."""
