summary: "Mule takes too long to start modules from agent (Sometimes more than 10s)"
description: """Currently, Studio is using the service """"/mule/tooling"""" which is a module in the agent, so we start checking the url until it is available in some cases it takes more than 10 seconds for the service to be up and running.  We should find a way of improving this experience."""
comments: "As discussed with the team, this will be tackle after GA release once the first minor release of Mule 4 is going to be released. Meanwhile, Studio will have in background a Mule Runtime instance that will be used for Tooling, if there are multiple versions of Mule 4.0.x Studio will choose the latest version to use for Tooling.  Nice to have, latest version defined by the open projects in Studio and the highest Mule Runtime version defined by this projects."

summary: "Classloading: define what dependencies should an ArtifactPluginDescriptor must have"
description: """Once MULE-11083 gets merged, those plugins that are consumed by the JSON descriptor will read the dependencies through the pom.xml file.    If the ArtifactPluginDescriptor gets injected with all the dependencies (not only mule plugins) the  org.mule.runtime.deployment.model.internal.plugin.BundlePluginDependenciesResolver.getArtifactsWithDependencies() will fail looking them up.    So we need to decide whether   (a) the descriptor will have *all* dependencies and filtrate them in the BundlePluginDependenciesResolver,   or  (b) the descriptor will have *just* the mule plugin dependencies and the BundlePluginDependenciesResolver keeps untouched.    I'd say (a) it's better, as further on, having all dependencies in the descriptor might be helpful for the framework testing."""

summary: "Classloading: provide support for dynamic class loader models"
description: """Once MULE-11083 gets merged, the `ArtifactPluginDescriptorFactory` object looks up into the mule-plugin.json descriptor for the classLoaderModelLoaderDescriptor element, which if exists, it will take the ID to see if it matches against the """"maven"""" model.    This method must be enhanced to provide a mechanism to load different types of IDs, such as the """"self-contained"""" model in which there won't be any pom.xml file to extract the dependencies (mainly used for testing purposes)"""
comments: "Fixed as part of MULE-11476"

summary: "Deployment tries to redeploy when an app has a missing plugin"
description: """When writing the following test       fails as the current deployer tries to redeploy the application when the plugin """"dependantPlugin"""" has a missing plugin in the classpath (in this sample, """"echoPlugin"""")    Current fix is:  """
comments: "Issue was fixed on MULE-11786" [SEP] "Test was updated on MULE-11786 and the test never failed on the CB... but the issue is still there :(.  The problem is that there is a race condition in the test. The assert is invoked right after starting the DeploymentDirectoryWatcher. The DeploymentDirectoryWatcher deploys everything and then starts the thread that checks for new deployments.  If the assertion is not immediately  invoked after the DeploymentDirectoryWatcher start, then it will receive tow notifications instead of one. There is a bug"

summary: "Improve ErrorModel serialization in ExtensionModels"
description: """*Improve ErrorModel serialization in ExtensionModels*    * Nowadays in an {{ExtensionModel}} the error models are serialized at *extension* level and also in each *operation* that declares it, not only serializing the error identifier, serializing the entire error hierarchy.  * Improve the serialization (like MetadataTypes) to have a normalized JSON where the error models from operations are just references to the errors defined at extension level."""

summary: "Calling getMuleContext on an Application that failed to deploy throws NPE"
description: """When calling getMuleContext on an application that failed to deploy it throws a NullPointerException because artifactContext is null."""
comments: "We can change it to throw a better exception, but you cnanot obtain a MuleContext for an application which failed to deploy. Fail to deploy == Couldn't create MuleContext" [SEP] "The behaviour in Mule 3.x is to return null. I think that would be a good compromise to maintain consistency across versions." [SEP] "we will change it so that:    * DefaultMuleApplication#getDomain() returns Optional<MuleContext>  * DefaultMuleApplication#getMuleContext() throws IllegalStateException is the application couldn't be deployed    However, notice that you should never call muleContext blindly. You should always be checking that application's status to make sure it was correctly deployed" [SEP] "Just put a clarification in the javadocs"
